title: Stairs
hints: Think about the base cases: how many ways to reach step 1? How many ways to reach step 2?
For step n, you can reach it from step (n-1) by taking 1 step, or from step (n-2) by taking 2 steps.
The number of ways to reach step n is the sum of ways to reach step (n-1) and step (n-2).
This is similar to the Fibonacci sequence.
Use dynamic programming to avoid recalculating the same subproblems.
Remember to take modulo 1000000007 at each step to prevent integer overflow.
